prompt: Why is failing to regenerate the session ID at login dangerous (servlet container)?
code_snippet:
// the session ID is NOT regenerated after a successful login
if (validLogin) { session.setAttribute("user", user); }

options.text: The password can be read directly from the session
The login form can be embedded in an iframe
An attacker who planted a known session ID before login can reuse it afterwards to hijack the now-authenticated session
The server runs out of sessions (denial of service)

explanation: If the ID stays the same across the login boundary, an attacker who fixed the victim's session ID beforehand is now inside the authenticated session — Session Fixation (CWE-384). Fix: regenerate the session ID on any privilege change (login).
source_reference: CWE-384; OWASP A07:2021 (Identification and Authentication Failures)

prompt: Why is failing to regenerate the session ID at login dangerous (framework default)?
code_snippet:

options.text: The session cookie becomes readable by JavaScript

The password can be read directly from the session
